items.title: IOS-XE Basic Troubleshooting and Configuration Guide

steps.action: Check device reachability and basic connectivity.
steps.expected: Successful ping to the device and/or gateway; interface shows line protocol up.

steps.action: Verify interface status and configuration.
steps.expected: Interface is administratively up/line protocol up with correct IP assignment.

steps.action: Check routing table and next-hop reachability.
steps.expected: Correct route present with active next-hop and outgoing interface.

steps.action: Validate static route if configured.
steps.expected: Static route appears in routing table with correct next-hop or exit interface.

steps.action: Inspect control-plane and management plane health.
steps.expected: CPU/memory within normal ranges; no critical alarms.

steps.action: Verify SSH/HTTPS management access and users.
steps.expected: SSH/HTTPS sessions established; authorized users present.

steps.action: Confirm system time and NTP sync.
steps.expected: Clock is synchronized; stratum reasonable.

steps.action: Check platform-specific features (FlexVPN/GETVPN/IoT).
steps.expected: Feature adjacencies formed; SA/Policy established as expected.

steps.action: Review diagnostics and health for hardware/software issues.
steps.expected: No critical hardware faults; software version matches expected image.

steps.action: Apply minimal config change and verify impact.
steps.expected: Change applied without disruption; desired reachability/behavior restored.

steps.action: Verify Nexus service status and basic connectivity.
steps.expected: Service is active and listening on expected ports; network reachability to the node is confirmed.

steps.action: Check Nexus logs for errors and warnings.
steps.expected: No critical errors; warnings are investigated and contextualized.

steps.action: Validate repository configuration and connectivity to upstream.
steps.expected: Repositories are correctly defined and can reach remote indexes; proxy settings (if any) are valid.

steps.action: Inspect blob store integrity and disk usage.
steps.expected: Blob store is healthy; disk usage is within acceptable thresholds.

steps.action: Review and adjust JVM and Nexus heap settings if needed.
steps.expected: Heap settings are appropriate for workload; no frequent GC pauses.

steps.action: Verify user roles, privileges, and authentication mechanisms.
steps.expected: Users/groups have correct roles; authentication (LDAP/Active Directory/OAuth) responds as expected.

steps.action: Test artifact upload/download to confirm repository operations.
steps.expected: Upload and download succeed; checksums and metadata are intact.

steps.action: Check and configure network/proxy settings if external resolution fails.
steps.expected: Outbound connections succeed via configured proxy or direct routing.

steps.action: Perform a controlled restart and monitor startup sequence.
steps.expected: Nexus starts without errors and becomes ready within expected time.

steps.action: Schedule and verify regular backups of configuration and blob store.
steps.expected: Backup completes successfully and restore tests validate integrity.

steps.action: Verify physical connectivity and power
steps.expected: WLC is powered on, LEDs indicate normal operation, and Ethernet link LEDs are active.

steps.action: Access the WLC CLI via console or SSH
steps.expected: Successful login to the WLC command-line interface.

steps.action: Check system status and health
steps.expected: No critical alarms; system uptime and resources (CPU, memory) within normal ranges.

steps.action: Verify network configuration (IP, gateway, VLANs)
steps.expected: Management interface has correct IP, subnet, and default gateway; VLANs are configured if needed.

steps.action: Check controller reachability and AP connectivity
steps.expected: WLC can reach other network devices; APs are joining and in RUN state.

steps.action: Review logs for errors or warnings
steps.expected: No critical or repeated errors; logs show normal operation.

steps.action: Validate wireless configuration (SSID, security, VLAN mapping)
steps.expected: SSID profiles are correctly configured and mapped to proper VLANs and policies.

steps.action: Check RF and channel configuration
steps.expected: Channels and power settings are optimal and non-conflicting.

steps.action: Verify Layer 3 roaming and mobility settings
steps.expected: Mobility groups and IP helpers are correctly set for inter-controller roaming.

steps.action: Save configuration and document changes
steps.expected: Running configuration saved to startup configuration; changes recorded.

items.title: PAN-OS Basic Troubleshooting and Configuration Guide

steps.action: Check device connectivity and management access.
steps.expected: Management interface is reachable and admin can log in.

steps.action: Verify PAN-OS software version and availability of updates.
steps.expected: Device is running a supported PAN-OS version.

steps.action: Check interface status and IP configuration.
steps.expected: Interfaces are operational (up/up) with correct IP addressing.

steps.action: Review system logs for critical events.
steps.expected: No critical or repeated error entries.

steps.action: Validate security policies and rule hits.
steps.expected: Policies reflect intended allow/deny logic with expected hit counts.

steps.action: Check routing tables and next-hop reachability.
steps.expected: Preferred route is active and next-hop is reachable.

steps.action: Confirm NAT rules and translation behavior.
steps.expected: NAT rules match traffic intent and show expected translated addresses.

steps.action: Test end-to-end connectivity and application flow.
steps.expected: Traffic flows as expected; no drops due to security rules or NAT.

steps.action: Commit changes and verify configuration synchronization in HA pairs.
steps.expected: Commit succeeds and devices are synchronized in HA passive/active mode.

steps.action: Verify physical connectivity and power.
steps.expected: Device is powered on, all LEDs indicate normal operation (no fault LEDs). Management interface has link.

steps.action: Check interface status and IP configuration.
steps.expected: Management interface has correct IP, netmask, gateway, and is administratively up.

steps.action: Test reachability of the device.
steps.expected: Ping to management IP from a host succeeds.

steps.action: Confirm Panorama connectivity (if applicable).
steps.expected: Device shows connected to Panorama with accepted template stack.

steps.action: Check software version and availability of updates.
steps.expected: Running a supported, non-EOL PAN-OS version with latest updates applied.

steps.action: Review system logs for errors.
steps.expected: No critical or repeated errors; traffic and threat logs reflect expected activity.

steps.action: Validate virtual router and routing table.
steps.expected: Default route or expected next-hop present; virtual router shows correct interface zones.

steps.action: Check security policies and rule hits.
steps.expected: Policies reflect intended allow/deny logic; hit counts increase with matching traffic.

steps.action: Verify NAT rules and address objects.
steps.expected: Source/destination NAT rules translate addresses as designed; no overlapping IPs.

steps.action: Confirm decryption and SSL/TLS settings.
steps.expected: Decryption policies, SSL Forward Proxy, or SSL Inbound Inspection are correctly configured with valid certificates.

steps.action: Check HA status (if HA is enabled).
steps.expected: Active/Passive or Active/Active state is stable with sync in progress or completed.

steps.action: Validate threat prevention and URL filtering.
steps.expected: Threat prevention profiles and URL filtering are applied to policies; signatures and categories are up to date.

steps.action: Run diagnostics and capture traffic if needed.
steps.expected: Diagnostics complete; packet captures show expected traffic flows and issues.

steps.action: Commit changes and monitor post-commit stability.
steps.expected: Commit succeeds without errors; device remains responsive and policies take effect.
